questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak's post on May 27, 2026, what is one of the simple criteria for adding new Farcaster validators?
options: Be in a geographical region different from the current set
Run a modified fork of snapchain
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak stated that the criteria for adding validators right now is to run snapchain, not be neynar, and be in a geographical region different from the current validator set.

questions.question: What is the name of the live consensus system powering Farcaster that validators must run?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak mentions snapchain as the live consensus system that Farcaster validators must run.

questions.question: Which Gnomie did Jesse Pollak claim in the Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary on April 21, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post from April 21, 2026, states: 'I claimed Scruffy Duskhollow in the Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary!'

questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak, when did the Neynar acquisition deal fully close?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak mentioned that the Neynar deal fully closed at the beginning of April.

questions.question: Which snapchain mainnet shard experienced a halt on April 11, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak tweeted on April 11, 2026, that snapchain mainnet shard 1 had halted at ~32M height.

questions.question: What shirt color and face pattern did Jesse Pollak say he would wear at /frontiertower in April 2026?

questions.question: Which virtual farming game channel did Jesse Pollak's kids request their own farms on in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted on February 15, 2026: 'My kids are now asking for their own /fruitling-valley farms'.

questions.question: What percent global p95 latency reduction did developer @mcilroyc achieve on Farcaster in late January 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post on January 30, 2026, highlighted a '50% less global p95 latency' improvement for @mcilroyc.

questions.question: Which platform offers daily reward credits to build on neynar.com/studio?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted on February 28, 2026: 'daily reward credits to build on neynar.com/studio'.

questions.question: What are the top two validator onboarding priorities for Farcaster, in order, according to Jesse Pollak's May 27, 2026 post?
options: High stake size and low latency
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak stated on May 27, 2026, that the top 2 priorities in order right now are 'geo and no majority' to prioritize stability.

questions.question: Why will the Farcaster team currently not onboard a validator running a modified fork of snapchain?
options: It creates verification risks from the outside and they lack dev capacity to audit it
The code is proprietary and closed-source
Forks are incompatible with the EVM layer
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak wrote: 'a validator running a fork... creates risk we don't currently have systems set up to verify from the outside and don't have the dev capacity to take on right now.'

questions.question: Whose outside pull request contributions to Farcaster were merged but subsequently edited and rewritten?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post on May 27, 2026, mentions: 'we've merged contributions that came through PRs from outside the core team, including from cassie.'

questions.question: What was the testing timeline in May 2026 for moving Farcaster validators to a different continent?
options: Testing a testnet node first, followed by a mainnet validator the next week
Moving both testnet and mainnet simultaneously in week one
Moving mainnet first, then testing on testnet
Testing a testnet node for a full month before any mainnet moves
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted on May 1, 2026: 'this week - we're testing moving one our testnet nodes to a different continent. next week - we'll move one of our farcaster mainnet validators over...'

questions.question: On April 23, 2026, Jesse Pollak requested feedback on which Farcaster Improvement Proposal (FIP)?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post from April 23, 2026, is: 'Looking for feedback or reviews on the Snapchain Signers FIP'.

questions.question: During the wallet balance outage on March 13, 2026, what was the known edge-case pricing issue?
options: Tokens with super low liquidity USDC pairs were not priced correctly
All ERC-20 tokens were showing a zero balance
USDC native balances were completely invisible
Gas tokens were priced at double their market value
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak reported a known edge case fix in progress: 'tokens with super low liquidity USDC pair' are not priced correctly.

questions.question: Why did Jesse Pollak expect network instability around 12:00 PM UTC on February 24, 2026?
options: Shard 0 was cutting over to the updated validator set
Shard 1 was migrating to a new database engine
Neynar's validation servers were undergoing scheduled hardware upgrades
They were deploying the Snapchain Signers FIP to mainnet
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post states: 'expecting some additional instability around 12:00 PM UTC on Feb 24 when shard 0 cuts over to the updated validator set.'

questions.question: Where did Jesse Pollak pull college all-nighters playing gaming titles like Dota or League of Legends?

Replacing the RPC provider for Base mainnet
Deploying new compression algorithms for media attachments
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted that they were making 'significant changes at the database layer on Farcaster's backend today between noon - 1PM PST' which could cause mild degradation.

Secure configuration of Environment Variables & secrets

questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted: 'Tom Riddle’s diary will no longer be fiction. ... LLMs/agents have matured to a point where they can role play young Voldemort flawlessly...'

questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak, what is step 1 in the 'Neynar way' of sending bugs to jail?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak outlined Step 1 of the Neynar way as 'Detailed linear ticket' on February 19, 2026.

Reopen the ticket for quality assurance

options: Early internet revolution media coverage vs. modern crypto reporting
EVM performance vs. traditional web databases
Traditional finance journalism vs. decentralized blogging
Web2 search engine growth vs. early Farcaster adoption
questions.explanation: The post shared a link to: 'Comparing early internet journalism to crypto journalism', highlighting how early media coverage of the internet revolution compares to modern crypto reporting.

questions.question: Why does Jesse Pollak believe that Farcaster decentralization must be done 'one variable at a time'?
options: To prioritize network stability and growth over rapid decentralization
Because of regulatory constraints on multi-region validators
Because of hardware supply chain delays for validator nodes
To maximize fee distribution to early testnet developers
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak explained that 'stability and growth of the network has to come first - a decentralized protocol that nobody uses doesn't serve anyone.'

questions.question: Regarding open-source contributions to Snapchain, what distinction did Jesse Pollak make between Cassie's PR and the deployed mainnet codebase?
options: Merged PRs are not a guarantee of 1:1 compatibility because the team edited and rewrote parts of the code
Cassie's PR was rejected because it used a modified version of snapchain
The team adopted Cassie's PR 1:1 without any modifications
Cassie's contributions are only active on testnet networks
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak stated that 'merged contributions aren't the same as a guaranteed-compatiblity... the codebase running on mainnet today is not a 1:1 copy of any external PR.'

options: 'when it comes to AI, you don't get a Detroit or a San Jose'
'AI is a global phenomenon without physical boundaries'
'the next Silicon Valley is decentralized on-chain'
'AI development will not centralize in traditional hardware hubs'
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post on April 12, 2026, quotes: 'when it comes to AI, you don't get a Detroit or a San Jose' along with a YouTube link.

A major reorg on the Base blockchain mainnet

A security vulnerability within the smart contract indices

questions.question: Why does Jesse Pollak claim that AI and coding agents have made software building feel as rewarding as playing video games?
options: They shortened the timespan of the effort-reward cycle
They introduced multiplayer capabilities to IDEs
They gamified the compilation process with leaderboard points
They allow automated deployment directly to virtual worlds
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak wrote: 'Coding agents have shortened the time span of the effort-reward cycle enough for it to feel as rewarding as a game today.'
